title: The Facilitator's Catechism

creators.name: Richard J. Cordes
creators.affiliation: COGSEC; Remotor Consulting Group; Atlantic Council GeoTech Center

creators.name: Daniel Ari Friedman
creators.affiliation: COGSEC; Remotor Consulting Group; University of California, Davis, Department of Entomology

description: This paper discusses the origins and evolution of Operations Orders from antiquity to modern times and the impact of Operations Orders on organizational sensemaking. Perspectives from research on Complexity Science, Organizational Psychology, High Reliability Organizations, Memetics, Logistics, Knowledge Management Systems, and Active Inference are used to consider the historical, contemporary, and future requirements and constraints of Operations Orders. Examples of traditional military Operations Orders and their civilian counterparts are detailed in context with their respective environments and requirements. Key characteristics of survivability, contemporary and future requirements, and current limitations of extant Operations Orders are addressed in order to inform the proposal of a new Operations Order format for use by Process Facilitators of military, intelligence, and civilian teams: the “Facilitator’s Catechism”.

methods.name: OPORD analysis and catechism-style process design
methods.description: Applied opord analysis and catechism-style process design approach

methods.name: Organizational sensemaking and high-reliability frameworks
methods.description: Applied organizational sensemaking and high-reliability frameworks approach

key_findings: Historical and futures-oriented analysis of operations orders (OPORD) for organizational sensemaking, leading to a catechism-style OPORD format for process facilitators in military, intelligence, and
